name: Sernia recoverable tool-error loop
description: Fires when a SINGLE Sernia agent conversation hits >=3 recoverable tool errors within 15 minutes — a signal that one run is stuck retrying (e.g. repeated workspace_edit_file EditErrors), which usually means the agent needs tuning or MEMORY.md/skill content has drifted. These errors are intentionally logged at warning level (below the error-level catch-all alert) because a single occurrence is self-correcting noise; this alert exists to catch the rapid-repeat case the downgrade would otherwise hide. Identified by span_name='sernia tool error: {tool_name}' with is_exception=true and level<17 (error level), which by construction is exactly the recoverable SandboxError family routed to warn by ErrorLoggingToolset.
query:
SELECT
    attributes->>'conversation_id' AS conversation_id,
    count(*) AS error_count,
    array_agg(DISTINCT r.exception_type) AS error_types,
    max(r.start_timestamp) AS last_seen,
    'https://logfire-us.pydantic.dev/espo412/portfolio'
        || '?q=attributes%5B%27conversation_id%27%5D%3D%27'
        || (attributes->>'conversation_id') || '%27'
        AS conversation_url
FROM records AS r
WHERE r.span_name = 'sernia tool error: {tool_name}'
  AND r.is_exception = true
  AND r.level < 17
  AND r.service_name = 'fastapi'
  AND (r.deployment_environment IS NULL OR r.deployment_environment != 'local')
  AND COALESCE(attributes->>'conversation_id', '') != ''
GROUP BY attributes->>'conversation_id'
HAVING count(*) >= 3
ORDER BY error_count DESC
LIMIT 20
